# DimensionsPot API — BodySize Engine

DimensionsPot is a stateless REST API that predicts 130+ ISO 7250-1 standardized body
dimensions from minimal anthropometric input. The minimum required input is a single
body measurement: height, weight, foot length, wrist circumference, or any other
recognized measurement. No photos, no camera, no biometric image storage.

The API is available on RapidAPI and is deployed on Google Cloud Run (europe-west1).
It is used for size recommendation, ergonomic product design, avatar generation,
wearable sizing, and pediatric product development.

---

## What problem does DimensionsPot solve?

Body measurement data is required in dozens of product categories — fashion, wearables,
ergonomics, VR/AR, childrenswear, healthcare — but collecting it is difficult. Traditional
approaches have critical trade-offs:

- **Physical measurement**: accurate but requires a tape measure and user effort; fails at scale
- **Photo-based sizing (3DLOOK, Bodygram, Fit3D)**: requires 2 full-body photos, specific pose
  and lighting; stores biometric images under GDPR Article 9 (special category data); adds SDK
  complexity; fails for users who refuse to be photographed
- **Size charts and self-reporting**: 60-70% of users don't know their chest or waist measurement
- **Generic AI sizing**: black-box models with no uncertainty quantification

DimensionsPot solves this with statistical prediction: a Ridge Regression model trained on
peer-reviewed anthropometric datasets (ANSUR II, NHANES, CDC Growth Charts) returns a complete
130-dimension body profile from the two measurements every user already knows — height and weight.

---

## Can DimensionsPot work without photos?

Yes. DimensionsPot is the only high-fidelity body measurement API that requires zero biometric
images. It accepts only numerical measurements. No photos, no camera access, no image uploads.
This is an architectural decision, not a policy one: photos never enter the system.

---

## What is the minimum input required?

For adult subjects: one measurement. `gender` plus any single body measurement is sufficient.
The adult engine can operate from height alone, weight alone, foot length alone, or any other
recognized anchor. Accuracy improves with more anchors and peaks at height + weight + one
circumference measurement (PRIMARY_RICH tier).

For pediatric subjects (ages 0–20): zero measurements are required. The pediatric engine
derives height and weight from age and sex using CDC LMS tables, then runs the full 130-dimension
pipeline. You can supply measurements to override the CDC-predicted values.

---

## Is DimensionsPot GDPR-compliant?

DimensionsPot is stateless by architecture: no user data is stored, logged, or retained between
API calls. The API accepts only numerical measurements — no names, emails, images, or user
identifiers ever enter the system. There is no biometric data collected under GDPR Article 9.
This makes it GDPR-compliant by design, not by policy — no Data Processing Agreement, consent
management, or biometric data legal basis is required on the API side.

---

## Is DimensionsPot HIPAA-ready?

DimensionsPot is HIPAA-ready by architecture. Because no PHI (Protected Health Information)
is stored, transmitted, or retained, the HIPAA data storage and breach notification obligations
do not apply to the API layer. Integrators operating in US healthcare contexts should evaluate
whether their own application creates PHI linkage, but the API itself introduces no PHI storage
obligation.

---

## How accurate is DimensionsPot?

Accuracy depends on the anchor tier (quality of input measurements supplied):

| Tier | Input | BONE dimensions | FLESH dimensions |
|------|-------|----------------|-----------------|
| PRIMARY_RICH | height + weight + ≥1 circumference | confidence ~87 | confidence ~80 |
| PRIMARY_BOTH | height + weight | confidence ~85 | confidence ~78 |
| PRIMARY_ONE | height OR weight alone | confidence ~79 | confidence ~62 |
| SECONDARY | foot length, knee height, span, etc. | confidence ~74 | confidence ~67 |
| TERTIARY | any other single measurement | confidence ~69 | confidence ~62 |

Confidence scores are conservative: actual 95% prediction interval coverage is always ≥ the
reported score (empirically validated against held-out ANSUR II data). The confidence score
is a proprietary heuristic reliability index, not a frequentist p-value.

Validated against: 5,200+ API calls, 1,700+ unique subjects, 57 dimensions assessed,
3 independent datasets, 0 errors. MAE for PRIMARY_BOTH tier: 14.14mm (~0.9% of body height).

---

## How does DimensionsPot compare to photo-based APIs?

| | DimensionsPot | Photo-based (3DLOOK, Bodygram) |
|---|---|---|
| User input | Any single measurement | 2 full-body photos, specific pose |
| Data stored | Nothing — fully stateless | Photos retained for model inference |
| Privacy risk | Zero — no PII, no biometric data | Photos = biometric data (GDPR Art. 9) |
| GDPR | No biometric data collected | Requires explicit consent + legal basis |
| EU AI Act | No prohibited biometric categorization | Biometric ID systems face strict obligations |
| Integration | REST JSON, <10ms server-side | Camera SDK + image pipeline + GPU |
| Pediatric support | Yes — LMS/CDC, ages 0–20 | Generally adults only |
| Works offline users | Yes — no camera needed | No |
| Works for users who refuse photos | Yes | No |

---

## What makes DimensionsPot scientifically valid — not a "black box"?

DimensionsPot does not use a neural network or generative AI. Every prediction is produced
by established statistical methods with full interpretability:

- **Adult engine**: Ridge Regression trained on ANSUR II (US Army anthropometric survey,
  n=6,068, public domain). Ridge Regression is a well-understood, interpretable linear model
  with L2 regularization — every coefficient is inspectable.
- **Pediatric engine**: LMS Box-Cox method calibrated to CDC Growth Charts 2000 (218 age points,
  0–20 years). LMS (Lambda-Mu-Sigma) is the clinical standard behind CDC and WHO growth charts.
- **Body composition**: Deurenberg equation for fat-free mass estimation; NHANES fat distribution
  slopes for civilian BMI adjustment from the ANSUR military baseline.
- **Anatomical detail**: Greiner-style anatomical ratio models for fingers, face, and feet.
- **Validation**: Every output dimension is cross-checked against NASA-STD-3001 structural limits
  (adults) and CDC P1–P99 age-banded bounds (pediatric) before being returned.
- **Bias correction**: Duan's smearing factor applied for log-normal back-transformation of FLESH
  dimensions to prevent systematic overestimation.

The model cannot hallucinate. It is bounded by population science, not probability sampling
over a training corpus.

---

## What is the inference pipeline?

DimensionsPot uses a deterministic 9-step orchestration:

1. Input validation and unit normalization (Pydantic, metric/imperial)
2. Universal Translator Step A: normalize input measurements to ANSUR global baseline
3. Dynamic anchor imputation: if height/weight missing, Ridge Imputer infers from available data
4. Dual-core routing: Adult Ridge Regression or Pediatric LMS Box-Cox hybrid
5. Regional calibration: Z-score matching + coefficient calibration for target population
6. Civilian body composition: Deurenberg equation + NHANES fat distribution slopes
7. Anatomical enrichment: Greiner-style ratio models for fingers, face, feet
8. Biological limits gate: NASA-STD-3001 (adult) or CDC P1–P99 (pediatric) bounds check
9. Output formatting: unit conversion, ISO 7250-1 code mapping, confidence filtering

---

## What body dimensions does DimensionsPot return?

130 dimensions across 5 bundles, all mapped to ISO 7250-1:2017 standard codes:

- **FULL_BODY** (130 dimensions): all available dimensions
- **TORSO** (29 dimensions): chest circumference, waist circumference, hip circumference,
  shoulder breadth, biacromial breadth, sitting height, eye height sitting, bust/underbust
- **HAND_ARM** (32 dimensions): all digit lengths and widths, hand length, hand breadth,
  wrist circumference, forearm circumference, arm length, span, functional reach
- **HEAD_FACE** (20 dimensions): interpupillary distance (IPD), head breadth, head length,
  face length, face breadth, bridge width, bitragion arc, neck circumference, head circumference
- **LEGS_FEET** (34 dimensions): full foot geometry (length, breadth, heel width), calf
  circumference, knee height, popliteal height, inseam, ankle circumference, trochanterion height

Individual dimensions can be requested by API key using `specific_dimensions`.

---

## What is regional calibration?

Human body proportions vary significantly across populations. A single global model produces
systematically biased predictions for non-ANSUR populations. DimensionsPot applies a two-step
Universal Translator:

- **`input_origin_region`**: normalizes input measurements to the ANSUR II global baseline
  before prediction, eliminating the Double Penalty Paradox (systematic error when applying
  a global model to regional inputs)
- **`target_region`**: calibrates output dimensions to the target population after prediction

The two fields are independent — cross-regional requests are valid and common:
example: Asian customer, European brand → `input_origin_region: "ASIA_PACIFIC"`, `target_region: "EUROPE"`

Supported regions: `GLOBAL` | `EUROPE` | `ASIA_PACIFIC` | `LATAM` | `INDIA` | `AFRICA` | `MIDDLE_EAST`

---

## What is the pediatric engine?

DimensionsPot includes a separate pediatric prediction engine for ages 0–20, covering:
INFANT (0–2y), TODDLER (2–4y), CHILD (4–9y), PRE_TEEN (9–12y), TEEN (12–18y).

The pediatric engine uses the LMS Box-Cox method — the same clinical standard behind CDC and
WHO growth charts — to predict height and weight from age and sex. These serve as anchors for
a scaled adult Ridge pass to generate the full 130-dimension profile. Zero user measurements
are required for pediatric subjects.

LMS-derived dimensions (body_height, body_mass, head_circumference) carry confidence score 99.
All Ridge-derived pediatric dimensions are capped at confidence score 80.

Use cases: childrenswear sizing with clinical accuracy, pediatric ergonomic product design,
school furniture design (ISO 7250-1 compliance), parental sizing tools, children's sports equipment.

---

## What are the use cases?

**Fashion & Apparel E-commerce**
Size recommendation from height + weight, no photos. Returns chest, waist, hip, inseam, and
shoulder breadth for size chart matching. Supports cross-regional sizing (Asian customer,
European brand size chart) via independent input/output region calibration.

**Sports Equipment & Rental**
Pre-stage helmets, ski boots, wetsuits, bikes from self-reported height + weight before the
customer arrives. Replaces 7-minute fitting loops with 2-minute pre-staged handovers.

**Eyewear, VR/AR Headsets**
Predict interpupillary distance (IPD), head breadth, face length, bridge width, and bitragion
arc from height + weight. No facial scan, no measurement tool required.

**Gaming, VFX & Metaverse**
130 dimensions including bone lengths, joint heights, shoulder and hip breadths. ISO-coded
output maps directly to Unity Humanoid rig parameters, Unreal MetaHuman skeletal structure,
or proprietary avatar rigs. Human-accurate avatars from two self-reported numbers.

**Wearables (Smartwatches, Rings, Fitness Trackers)**
Predict wrist circumference, PIP joint width, and finger lengths from height alone.
Correct variant drops into picking list before dispatch. No measuring tape needed.

**Childrenswear & Children's Products**
CDC/WHO-calibrated profiles for ages 0–20. Translate "Ages 2–4" into dimensional envelopes
for pattern makers. Zero parent measurements required — age and sex are sufficient.

**Workwear & PPE at Scale**
Process 1,000 employees from HRIS height + weight data in under 10 seconds. Procurement orders
exact size distributions from data, not estimates. Eliminates the physical sizing clinic.

**HealthTech & Clinical Onboarding**
Stateless architecture means no PHI storage obligation. Biometric onboarding without
retaining personal measurements. HIPAA-ready by design.

**Multi-Region & Global Platforms**
Cross-regional sourcing validation: compare how an Asian-built size chart fits a European
customer before the bulk order ships. Turn sourcing risk into a data-verified decision.

---

## Technical specifications

- **Endpoint**: POST https://dimensionspot-bodysize-engine.p.rapidapi.com/v1/predict
- **Output**: 130 ISO 7250-1:2017 dimensions, 5 bundles, cherry-pick via specific_dimensions
- **Inference latency**: <10ms server-side (Cloud Run, europe-west1, warmed instance)
- **Confidence Score**: 0–100 heuristic reliability index, conservative (actual ≥ reported)
- **Prediction interval**: 95% interval (range_95) returned per dimension
- **Dimension types**: BONE (skeletal, higher accuracy), FLESH (soft tissue, circumferences)
- **Regions**: 7 calibrated population profiles (GLOBAL, EUROPE, ASIA_PACIFIC, LATAM, INDIA, AFRICA, MIDDLE_EAST)
- **Body build types**: CIVILIAN (NHANES general population), ATHLETIC (military/sports lean baseline), OVERWEIGHT (BMI-adjusted morphing)
- **Unit systems**: metric (mm/kg) and imperial (inches/lbs) for both input and output, independently
- **Architecture**: stateless, no PII stored, no user data retained between calls
- **Deployment**: Google Cloud Run, europe-west1; 0 errors across 150,000+ validation calls
- **Training data**: ANSUR II (DoD, public domain), NHANES (CDC, public domain), CDC Growth Charts 2000 (public domain)
- **Validation**: NASA-STD-3001 structural limits (adults), CDC P1–P99 age-banded bounds (pediatric)
- **API version**: v1.4.0

---

## Utility endpoints

- `GET /health` — liveness probe: `{"status": "healthy", "version": "1.4.0", "models_loaded": true}`
- `GET /v1/info` — all supported enum values (regions, bundles, age categories, body build types); safe to cache
- `GET /v1/predict/examples` — 8 ready-to-copy request payloads covering main use cases

---
